title: acer laurinum subsp. garrettii
intro: Acer laurinum subsp. garrettii, also known as the Laurel Maple, is a subspecies of the Maple tree that is native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are obtained from its leaves and bark. The herbal preparations of Laurel Maple, such as decoctions and infusions, are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is used in Ayurvedic medicine and is also used in some herbal remedies to treat various ailments, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
